page: Microsoft Azure F48amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture and a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPUs are present in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The server achieves top-decile results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as PassMark CPU and database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads and large-block writes ranking in the top 10%, while large-block uncached reads place in the top 25%. For data manipulation, both gzip compression and bzip3 decompression operate in the top tier.

While the server excels at processing smaller workloads, such as Redis SET operations exceeding 130 million operations per second and LLM inference for small and medium models, it exhibits a performance tradeoff on larger models. LLM inference for large 70B models drops to average tier speeds. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio, dedicated compute cores, and substantial local storage, this server is configured for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, and enterprise web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure F48amds_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine configured with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 5669 GB of local storage. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth. It delivers exceptional throughput for Redis operations and small-to-medium LLM inference, though performance drops to average levels for large 70B parameter models. Lacking a GPU, it relies entirely on its high-frequency AMD processor and dense memory configuration. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-heavy databases, intensive data processing, and high-throughput web serving where massive local storage and dedicated CPU resources are required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48amds_v7 is a memory-intensive server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48amds_v7 is a memory-intensive server with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage for database and compute workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive x86_64 platform featuring forty-eight dedicated AMD EPYC cores, massive local storage, and top-tier database processing performance.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
384 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier performance in database operations and Redis throughput
Average-tier performance for large-scale 70B LLM inference workloads
Optimized for memory-intensive databases and large-scale data processing
